     The Numbers Business Report

Megalopolis (2024)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $7,629,085Details
International Box Office $4,674,207
Worldwide Box Office $12,303,292
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A Roman Epic fable set in an imagined Modern America. The City of New Rome must change, causing conflict between Cesar Catilina, a genius artist who seeks to leap into a utopian, idealistic future, and his opposition, Mayor Franklyn Cicero, who remains committed to a regressive status quo, perpetuating greed, special interests, and partisan warfare. Torn between them is socialite Julia Cicero, the mayor’s daughter, whose love for Cesar has divided her loyalties, forcing her to discover what she truly believes humanity deserves.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$4,007,797 (52.5% of total gross)
Legs:1.90 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:62.0% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Theater counts:1,854 opening theaters/1,854 max. theaters, 2.2 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $8,067,534

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: September 27th, 2024 (Wide) by Lionsgate
September 27th, 2024 (IMAX) by Lionsgate
International Releases: September 25th, 2024 (Wide) (France)
September 26th, 2024 (Wide) (Austria)
September 26th, 2024 (Wide) (Australia)
September 26th, 2024 (Wide) (Germany)
September 26th, 2024 (Limited) (New Zealand)
Video Release: November 12th, 2024 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for sexual content, nudity, drug use, language and some violence.
(Rating bulletin 2840 (cert #55056), 6/26/2024)
Running Time: 138 minutes
Film festival: Cannes Film Festival 2024
Filming location (country): Italy, Switzerland, United States
Filming location (state or county): Georgia, United States
Plot point: Architecture, Government Corruption, Time Travel
Release detail: Disrupted by 2020 COVID-19 pandemic, Festival Acquisition
Social setting: Dystopia, Film Noir, Utopia
Subgenre: Political Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: American Zoetrope, Caesar Film LLC, Lionsgate
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A Roman Epic fable set in an imagined Modern America. The City of New Rome must change, causing conflict between Cesar Catilina, a genius artist who seeks to leap into a utopian, idealistic future, and his opposition, Mayor Franklyn Cicero, who remains committed to a regressive status quo, perpetuating greed, special interests, and partisan warfare. Torn between them is socialite Julia Cicero, the mayor’s daughter, whose love for Cesar has divided her loyalties, forcing her to discover what she truly believes humanity deserves.
